VERDICT: REFUSE — nested KVM is unavailable on this Mac. QEMU TCG stays the only local ARM oracle until the Ampere Altra box (hm-7pb) arrives.

HOST FACTS (2026-07-15):
- Chip: Apple M1 Max (hw.model MacBookPro18,2) — M1 generation
- macOS: 26.4.1 (build 25E253) — satisfies the macOS 15+ gate
- arch: arm64

GATE HIT: Apple's Virtualization.framework nested virtualization requires an M3-or-later chip AND macOS 15+. The macOS gate passes (26.4.1 >> 15) but the CHIP gate FAILS — M1 Max is two generations below M3. Hardware limit; no software workaround exists.

EVIDENCE — authoritative host-API check (not just chip-name inference):
  $ cat nestcheck.swift
  import Virtualization
  if #available(macOS 15.0, *) {
      print("isNestedVirtualizationSupported = \(VZGenericPlatformConfiguration.isNestedVirtualizationSupported)")
  }
  $ swift nestcheck.swift
  VZGenericPlatformConfiguration.isNestedVirtualizationSupported = false

Because macOS 26.4.1 already provides the nested-virt API, the `false` return is the framework reporting that the M1 hardware cannot do nested virtualization — the definitive answer straight from the platform, on this exact machine.

REFUSAL POINT / METHOD: Stopped at method step 1 (host-facts) per the spec's step-3 rule ("pre-M3 chip -> STOP, record REFUSE, do not chase workarounds"). No VM bring-up attempted: the host framework already reports nested virt unsupported, so any aarch64 Linux guest here would fall back to TCG and could never expose a hardware-accelerated /dev/kvm. There is no ioctl transcript because the /dev/kvm loop is physically unreachable on this hardware.

CONSEQUENCE for hm-cbt (ARM backend skeleton): the local KVM ioctl/boot dev loop is NOT available on this Mac. Pure ARM logic still runs natively (the Mac is aarch64), but the KVM ioctl loop (KVM_CREATE_VM / KVM_CREATE_VCPU / KVM_RUN) requires the Altra box (hm-7pb). QEMU TCG is the sanctioned fallback oracle for the ioctl/boot path until then.

INSTALLS / REVERTS: NONE. swift/swiftc were already present (/usr/bin via CommandLineTools); no brew/lima/UTM installs. Zero box time, zero spend, zero repo working-tree changes.

Box-dispatched nightly GREEN (run 29444065376, 2026-07-15, foreman-dispatched): campaign-runner full-lib Miri step success inside the unsafe-crates job (63.4 min total vs 155 ceiling); vmm-core Miri job success 45.8 min. The demonstrated-budget confirmation this bead waited for; runner's broken stable toolchain (hm-ph7) did not touch the pinned-nightly jobs.
